sections.title: Market Pulse: Jobs Report Cools Rate Hike Fears, Tech Under Pressure

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Mixed as Dow Rallies on Jobs Data, Nasdaq Slips Amid Tech Sell-off
items.summary: The U.S. stock market experienced a mixed session on Thursday, July 2, 2026. The Dow Jones Industrial Average surged, benefiting from a cooler-than-expected June jobs report that eased concerns about aggressive interest rate hikes. However, the Nasdaq Composite faced headwinds, trading lower due to persistent valuation trims and a sell-off in semiconductor and AI-related stocks. Overall market sentiment was generally higher, with investors rotating out of high-flying tech sectors.

items.statContext: Up +0.24% (+18.27 points). Initially up 0.49%, later falling 0.3%.
Down -0.18% (-46.96 points). Later reported as 1.1% lower.
Up +0.75% (+392.58 points), marching towards its 20th record close of the year. Also reported up 0.6% (+329 points).

items.headline: Micron Technology ($MU) Plunges as Chip Sell-off Continues
items.summary: Memory maker Micron Technology extended its losses, dropping significantly as the semiconductor sector faced continued pressure.

items.headline: June Jobs Report: Payrolls Miss Expectations, Unemployment Rate Dips to 4.2%
items.summary: The U.S. Labor Department reported that nonfarm payrolls for June rose by a seasonally adjusted 57,000 jobs, falling significantly short of economists' expectations ranging from 100,000 to 115,000. Prior months' figures were also revised lower. The unemployment rate edged down to 4.2% from 4.3%, although this was primarily attributed to a decrease in labor force participation. Average hourly earnings increased by 0.3% month-over-month and 3.5% year-over-year. This weaker-than-expected jobs data has tempered expectations for further interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve, with traders now seeing an 80-82% chance that the Fed will not raise rates at its next meeting.

items.headline: Tech Sector Faces Continued Sell-off Amid Valuation Concerns and AI Overcapacity Worries
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly chip stocks, continued to experience a sell-off today. Investors are expressing concerns about high valuations and potential overcapacity in the artificial intelligence (AI) computing space, particularly after reports of Meta Platforms potentially selling excess AI computing power. This has led to a rotation out of historically high-flying tech stocks into other asset classes, impacting indices like the Nasdaq Composite.

items.headline: Oil Prices Decline on Hopes for Peace, Bitcoin Rises on Easing Inflation Fears
items.summary: In the commodities market, Brent crude oil prices continued to sink, falling to around $70.68-$70.78 per barrel, driven by hopes for negotiations for a permanent end to the conflict with Iran. Conversely, Bitcoin, the world's largest cryptocurrency, surged higher, with prices above $61,275, as easing inflation concerns provided a boost to risk assets.
